name: THECA CELL TUMOR OF OVARY
definition: THECOMA = FIBROSED THECOMA = FIBROTHECOMA benign sex cord-stromal tumor similar to fibroma rare subtype of fibrothecoma containing steroid type cells

demographics.Path: solid mass, occasionally with cystic changes
demographics.Histo: spectrum of tumors with varying amounts of theca cells (= swollen lipid-rich stromal
demographics.Frequency: 0.5-1% of all ovarian neoplasms; 50% of all gonadal-stromal tumors

dominant_finding: hypointense ovarian mass on T2WI ← fibrotic component
discriminating_features: hypointense ovarian mass on T2WI ← fibrotic component
high SI on T2WI for components with little / no fibrosis ← edema / cystic degeneration of
variable degree of contrast enhancement governed by amount of nonenhancing fibrous tissue
widening of endometrial stripe (= thickened endometrium)

US: echogenic mass with sound attenuation / well-defined hypoechoic mass / anechoic lesion
MR: hypointense ovarian mass on T2WI ← fibrotic component
high SI on T2WI for components with little / no fibrosis ← edema / cystic degeneration of
variable degree of contrast enhancement governed by amount of nonenhancing fibrous tissue
widening of endometrial stripe (= thickened endometrium)

clinical.Prognosis: almost always benign with low malignant potential
